ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields are legible. Certificate number SEG-26D-466305, marked ORIGINAL (1/1).

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible. Invoice No. 11428 dated 26.01.2026. Issued by Global Forest Products Sweden AB.

lc_validation.explanation: The certificate's Remarks field (box 5) contains 'L/C NO. 151591LC25000010'. 'L/C NO.' is a separate field label that constitutes a recognized bank-term designation for Letter of Credit. The number '151591LC25000010' follows this label. Additionally, the invoice confirms 'PAYMENT TERMS: LETTER OF CREDIT' and references the same 'L/C NO. 151591LC25000010' in the goods description. The LC exception is therefore activated per section 2.2.

locations_found: Invoice page 1: Header/branding area – 'GLOBAL FOREST PRODUCTS' logo text
Invoice page 1: Footer issuer block – 'GLOBAL FOREST PRODUCTS SWEDEN AB'
Invoice page 1: Signature area at bottom – 'GLOBAL FOREST PRODUCTS SWEDEN AB'
Invoice page 1: VAT block – 'VAT NO. SE559374366801' associated with the same company

pairing_validation.explanation: Certificate references invoice no. 11428-2. Invoice number is 11428. The certificate's invoice reference '11428-2' contains the invoice number '11428' as a base reference with a suffix '-2' (likely a shipment or revision number). Consignor 'GLOBAL FOREST PRODUCTS SWEDEN AB' is explicitly identified in the invoice footer. Consignee 'SHANDONG PUBLISHING FOREIGN TRADE COMPANY LIMITED' is identified in the invoice's BUYER field. At least all three criteria are met. Document pair is valid.

comparison_steps.action: Identify explicit origin field in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Found 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN' in goods description area of invoice
Certificate: 'SWEDEN' → normalized 'Sweden'. Invoice: 'SWEDEN' → normalized 'Sweden'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.action: Verify explicit origin field requirement per section 2.2 LC rule
comparison_steps.result: The invoice contains a dedicated 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN' field in the goods description, which is an explicit origin field. Requirement met.

rules_applied.description: LC exception – only country of origin is verified
Identification of origin in invoice – 'Country of Origin' field found
Normalization of country names – exact match after case normalization

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate box 3 states origin as 'SWEDEN'. Invoice contains an explicit 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN' field in the goods description area. After normalization, both values are 'Sweden' — exact match. The LC exception requires origin to be found in an explicit origin field, which is satisfied here.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'SWEDEN' (CD-007). Invoice origin: 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN' (IV-007). The invoice field is a dedicated origin declaration, meeting the LC exception's requirement for an explicit origin field per section 2.2. Normalization: uppercase to title case. Result: identical values. No other origin fields in the invoice contradict this. Single origin country in certificate, single origin in invoice. MATCH with high confidence.

evidence.location: Goods description area, line 2 of product description

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: LC exception – Letter of Credit check
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: LC exception activated. Certificate contains 'L/C NO. 151591LC25000010' with a separate field label 'L/C NO.' which is a recognized bank-term designation. Invoice confirms payment terms as 'LETTER OF CREDIT' and the same LC number. Only country of origin is verified; all other checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE.

Document A identified as certificate (Certificate of Origin, ICC/EU format). Document B identified as invoice (Commercial Invoice No. 11428). Certificate references invoice 11428-2; invoice number is 11428. Pairing established.

PASS. All three criteria met: (1) Invoice number base '11428' matches certificate reference '11428-2'; (2) Consignor 'GLOBAL FOREST PRODUCTS SWEDEN AB' found in invoice footer; (3) Consignee 'SHANDONG PUBLISHING FOREIGN TRADE COMPANY LIMITED' found in invoice BUYER field.

rule_evaluation_log.rule_description: Country of origin – identification in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH. 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN' found as explicit origin field in invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: LC exception should not be activated because '151591LC25000010' contains 'LC' as part of the number
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The LC reference in the certificate is 'L/C NO. 151591LC25000010'. The field label 'L/C NO.' is a separate, explicit bank-term designation that appears before the number. It is not merely the letters 'LC' embedded within a reference number. The field label clearly identifies this as a Letter of Credit reference. Per section 2.2, 'L/C' is a recognized designation. The invoice additionally confirms 'PAYMENT TERMS: LETTER OF CREDIT' which corroborates the LC nature of this transaction.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice number mismatch (11428 vs 11428-2) should invalidate the document pair
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The certificate references 'COMMERCIAL INVOICE NO. 11428-2' while the invoice is numbered '11428'. The '-2' suffix likely denotes a shipment number, partial shipment, or revision. All other identifying data (consignor, consignee, vessel, port, LC number, contract number, goods, quantities) match exactly between the documents. The base invoice number '11428' is clearly the same document. Under the LC exception, invoice number verification is not a required checkpoint. The pairing validation at 8.6 is satisfied since the base invoice number matches and both consignor and consignee are identifiable.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet innehåller en Letter of Credit-referens, vilket innebär att enbart ursprungsland verifieras. Ursprungslandet Sverige anges i certifikatet och bekräftas uttryckligen i fakturan med 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN'. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: LC exception activated. Only country of origin verified. Certificate states origin 'SWEDEN'; invoice contains explicit 'COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: SWEDEN'. Exact match. All other checkpoints are NOT_APPLICABLE under LC exception. Document pair is IDENTICAL.
